name: SK Hynix
summary: SK Hynix is the world's #2 memory chip producer. **SK HYNIX Q1 CALL DISCLOSED 'SUFFICIENT HELIUM INVENTORY' + DIVERSIFIED SUPPLIERS (CNBC APRIL 23). SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX SECURED LONG-TERM US HELIUM CONTRACTS (DIGITIMES APRIL 9). THE KORNBLUTH 60-DAY PRODUCTION-CUT THESIS IS MATERIALLY CONSTRAINED.** SK Hynix said on its Q1 earnings call that it has 'diversified suppliers for key industrial inputs such as helium, bromine, and tungsten and has built up sufficient inventory, with limited impact expected on its production.' Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests with June supply ramp (Digitimes / Trendforce) — adjacent validation that the helium-mitigation channel is operational. **Capacity-zero constraint persists** — Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon/Google making unprecedented financing proposals. **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** Memory shortage expected to last through 2027 or 2030. **SMH +2.1% 1D / +20.3% 1M / +40.4% 3M** on Nvidia Q1 earnings + helium-mitigation thesis. **Helium clock Day 7 of 14** — the immediate-60-day production-cut path Kornbluth identified is now the LESS likely branch; the mitigation-through-inventory-and-substitution path is the MORE LIKELY branch. **Falsifiable**: SK Hynix or Samsung announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = Kornbluth thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A10 SK HYNIX TRADES AT ₩1.836M (+0.93% Monday) — Brokerages (Eugene Investment, Mirae Asset) maintained ₩3.2M target prices and ₩10,000 KOSPI outlook; KOSPI +0.6% rally Monday confirms the Friday -6.1% break was the panic, not the trend.** Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits upside. **Helium clock Day 3 of 14 (TrendForce 'two-week clock'); ~11 days remain at full production rates per the March 24 framework. SK Hynix capacity-zero + Samsung 18-day strike May 21 + helium clock = three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** Samsung HBM4 announcement Monday is competitor-leadership signal but **SK Hynix maintains relative-strength position within memory complex** (brokerages reiterating ₩3.2M targets). **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — operative price is Friday May 15 close (SK Hynix -7.7%). Helium-clock-expiry window NOW DAY 2 OF 14.** **Samsung 18-day strike May 21 — 4 days from start, no new resolution development over the weekend.** Korean fab complex three-simultaneous-supply-side-stressors continue: helium scarcity, Samsung strike from May 21, capacity-zero. Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits the upside. **PRIOR (May 16):** SK Hynix is the world's #2 memory chip producer and the dominant HBM (high-bandwidth memory) supplier for AI accelerators. Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI weight. **JPMorgan estimates Samsung strike losses up to ₩43T ($28.8B); SK Hynix sits adjacent to the strike-window May 21-June 7 with capacity-zero constraint** — memory order-shift to SK Hynix and Micron flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence; could benefit SK Hynix marginally on order rotation but capacity-zero constraint limits upside. **Helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from May 16; ~8 days remain at full production rates per March 24 framework). **SK Hynix capacity-zero + Samsung 18-day strike + helium clock-expiry stack is now operationally synchronised — three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** PRIOR (May 15): **A9 SK HYNIX AVAILABLE CAPACITY 'ESSENTIALLY ZERO'** per insider reporting (CONFIRMED — Tom's Hardware, TradingKey, Wccftech, ad-hoc-news, Seoul Economic Daily, Bloomberg). Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon and Google making unprecedented financing proposals to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines (each running hundreds of millions of dollars). **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** SK Hynix is cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at potentially discounted prices could crimp future profitability. **Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030.** SK Hynix +144% YTD into May 14. SK Hynix has publicly claimed 'diversified supplies' on helium (CLAIMED — one-sided, NOT independently corroborated; TrendForce data-driven analysis is REPORTED). **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23 and Samsung 18-day strike from May 21, SK Hynix sits at the center of the Korean fab complex's three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** **NEW TRIGGER**: 'SK Hynix announces supply guidance cut within 60 days' — would land helium-tsunami thesis. **Memory order-shift risk to SK Hynix and Micron** flagged by TrendForce as Samsung strike consequence — could BENEFIT SK Hynix marginally on order rotation, but capacity-zero constraint limits the upside.

active_triggers.condition: SK Hynix announces supply guidance cut or formal production halt within 60 days
SK Hynix accepts hyperscaler financing (Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon/Google) within 90 days — would confirm capacity-zero structural reality

recent_signals.headline: **SK HYNIX TARGETING 16-LAYER HBM4 MASS PRODUCTION BY Q3 — HELIUM DEPENDENCY EMBEDDED IN PRODUCTION RAMP; HELIUM CLOCK DAY 4 UNCHANGED.** SK Hynix relative-strength inside memory complex continues as the Samsung-strike-risk + AI-redistribution overhang weighs on Samsung specifically. **The HBM4 ramp into Feb-Mar 2026 mass-production makes the helium constraint operationally testable within 60 days** — if SK Hynix or Samsung announces production cut guidance within 60 days, Kornbluth thesis confirms; if spot helium retraces to pre-war ($120/MC or below) by July 1, thesis fails.
recent_signals.verification: SK Hynix 16-layer HBM4 Q3 target = REPORTED (Digitimes); helium clock Day 4 = CONFIRMED countdown; SK Hynix relative strength = CONFIRMED pattern.

Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output. SK Hynix cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at discounted prices could crimp future profitability. **Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030.** SK Hynix +144% YTD into May 14. recent_signals.verification: Capacity 'essentially zero' = CONFIRMED (multi-source insider reporting Tom's Hardware + Wccftech + TradingKey + ad-hoc-news + Seoul Economic Daily + Bloomberg); hyperscaler financing proposals = CONFIRMED ACTION (Tom's Hardware primary); memory shortage through 2027-2030 = REPORTED (analyst estimate); SK Hynix 'diversified supplies' on helium = CLAIMED (one-sided).
